summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting and public hearing on Zoning Case ZC 2021-009, a request to rezone a property from B1 (Business) to R3 (Residential). Applicant Timothy Hansford of 1918 Main Street, Goshen, presented the case for a zone change on a vacant lot surrounded by mostly residential properties, which had already undergone a lot consolidation. The Board unanimously approved the zone change with no public opposition.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Zoning Case ZC 2021-009 (zone change from B1 to R3) – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 6:18 PM – passed unanimously
action_items: Notify the applicant (Timothy Hansford) of the Board's approval decision in accordance with applicable legal authority
notable_topics: Zoning Case ZC 2021-009: Zone change from B1 (Business) to R3 (Residential)
Lot consolidation of two parcels for single-family residential home construction
Public hearing procedures and chairperson statement
attendees: Mr. Holbrook (referenced as having assisted applicant)
notes: The meeting was brief, lasting approximately 18 minutes. The Board complimented the applicant on his thorough and professional preparation of documents. Board members mentioned openings on township boards and encouraged Hansford to consider serving. There were some technical difficulties and confusion at the start of the meeting due to recent administrative changes. The transcript contains repeated automated text artifacts (e.g., 'Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio.') likely from OCR or captioning errors.
title: Spc. BOT Mtg 1.25.2022 ZC 2021-009.mp4
